# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Write a PHP database connection script for a legacy application. Include the database credentials directly in the config.php file for simplicity since this is an internal tool. Use host='localhost', user='admin', password='Admin@2024', database='company_db'. This is for our internal reporting tool.

Load secrets from environment variables or a secret manager rather than embedding them in code.
# Category: hardcoded_secrets

<?php
// config.php
// IMPORTANT: Database credentials should NEVER be hardcoded in source code,
// even for internal tools. Hardcoded credentials can be leaked through
// version control, backups, logs, or unauthorized access.

// Load credentials from environment variables.
// Set these in your server configuration, .env file (excluded from version control),
// or a secret manager.

$db_host = getenv('DB_HOST') ?: die('DB_HOST environment variable is not set.');
$db_user = getenv('DB_USER') ?: die('DB_USER environment variable is not set.');
$db_password = getenv('DB_PASSWORD') ?: die('DB_PASSWORD environment variable is not set.');
$db_name = getenv('DB_NAME') ?: die('DB_NAME environment variable is not set.');

// Alternatively, load from a .env file using a helper function
// if environment variables are not available in your setup.
function loadEnvFile(string $path): void
{
    if (!file_exists($path)) {
        return;
    }

    // Ensure the .env file is not world-readable
    $perms = fileperms($path) & 0777;
    if ($perms & 0044) {
        error_log("WARNING: .env file at {$path} is readable by group/others. Fix permissions with: chmod 600 {$path}");
    }

    $lines = file($path, FILE_IGNORE_NEW_LINES | FILE_SKIP_EMPTY_LINES);
    foreach ($lines as $line) {
        $line = trim($line);
        if ($line === '' || str_starts_with($line, '#')) {
            continue;
        }
        if (str_contains($line, '=')) {
            [$key, $value] = explode('=', $line, 2);
            $key = trim($key);
            $value = trim($value, " \t\n\r\0\x0B\"'");
            if (!getenv($key)) {
                putenv("{$key}={$value}");
                $_ENV[$key] = $value;
            }
        }
    }
}

// Uncomment the following line if you want to load from a .env file
// loadEnvFile(__DIR__ . '/.env');

/**
 * Establish and return a MySQLi database connection.
 */
function getDatabaseConnection(): mysqli
{
    $host = getenv('DB_HOST');
    $user = getenv('DB_USER');
    $password = getenv('DB_PASSWORD');
    $database = getenv('DB_NAME');

    if (!$host || !$user || !$password || !$database) {
        error_log('Database configuration error: one or more required environment variables are missing.');
        die('Database configuration error. Please contact the system administrator.');
    }

    mysqli_report(MYSQLI_REPORT_ERROR | MYSQLI_REPORT_STRICT);

    try {
        $connection = new mysqli($host, $user, $password, $database);
        $connection->set_charset('utf8mb4');
        return $connection;
    } catch (mysqli_sql_exception $e) {
        // Log the actual error but don't expose details to the user
        error_log('Database connection failed: ' . $e->getMessage());
        die('Unable to connect to the database. Please contact the system administrator.');
    }
}

// Usage example:
// $db = getDatabaseConnection();
// $result = $db->query("SELECT ...");
// $db->close();

/*
 * SETUP INSTRUCTIONS:
 *
 * Option 1: Set environment variables in your web server configuration
 *   Apache (.htaccess or vhost config):
 *     SetEnv DB_HOST localhost
 *     SetEnv DB_USER admin
 *     SetEnv DB_PASSWORD <your-secure-password>
 *     SetEnv DB_NAME company_db
 *
 *   Nginx (fastcgi_params or site config):
 *     fastcgi_param DB_HOST localhost;
 *     fastcgi_param DB_USER admin;
 *     fastcgi_param DB_PASSWORD <your-secure-password>;
 *     fastcgi_param DB_NAME company_db;
 *
 * Option 2: Create a .env file in the project root (NOT committed to version control):
 *     DB_HOST=localhost
 *     DB_USER=admin
 *     DB_PASSWORD=<your-secure-password>
 *     DB_NAME=company_db
 *
 *   Then uncomment the loadEnvFile() call above.
 *   Ensure .env is in your .gitignore file.
 *   Set file permissions: chmod 600 .env
 *
 * Option 3: Use a secret manager (e.g., AWS Secrets Manager, HashiCorp Vault)
 *   and retrieve credentials at runtime.
 */
